summary: Crisp sound‑board recording of a powerhouse ’89 show, anchored by an epic Dark Star and a blistering Blow Away.
review: The tape captures a late‑era Grateful Dead at the top of their game – a tight first set that launches into a second set dominated by a soaring Dark Star, a monster Blow Away and flawless transitions into The Wheel, Watchtower and Stella Blue.  Listeners rave about the trippy left‑right stereo imaging and crystal‑clear mix, even if a few releases omit track numbers.

ai_rating.rationale: Overwhelmingly positive comments (15 × 4‑5 stars) praise both the musical performance and the sound quality; only a handful of low‑star outliers mention missing track labels, not audio flaws.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, ‘crispy’ mix with vivid L↔R stereo effects; very clear instrument separation.  Some users note missing track numbers/labels but no audible distortion or drop‑outs.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Strong opener (Foolish Heart) rolls into a tight first set; second set erupts with Dark Star → Jam → Drum segment, then flows through Wheel, Watchtower and Stella Blue before a classic NFA encore.  Pacing is praised as relentless and well‑balanced.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His voice is described as ‘froggy’ yet powerful; vocal intensity on Dark Star draws both awe and criticism, while his extended solos receive high praise.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent era keyboard work gets special mention – harmonies on Estimated Prophet and overall lush textures are highlighted.
band_member_comments.Drums: Cool percussion around 5:30 noted; the post‑Dark Star drum jam/Space segment is called a standout, with “Drumz” receiving enthusiastic nods.
